jordan.title: DarkTrace Backup Setup
jordan.notes: Configure backups for the DarkTrace server to ensure config and threat data can be restored if the appliance fails.

subtasks.title: Identify what needs backing up (config, threat intel, packet captures, logs)
Determine backup destination (NAS, Veeam, off-site)
Configure scheduled backups on the DarkTrace appliance
Test backup restore to verify integrity
Document backup schedule and restore procedure

jordan.title: CIS 2.2 - Ensure Authorized Software Is Currently Supported
jordan.notes: Inventory all installed software and ensure only vendor-supported versions are in use. Remove or upgrade end-of-life software.

subtasks.title: Build inventory of all installed software across endpoints and servers
Identify end-of-life or unsupported software
Create upgrade/removal plan for unsupported software
Execute upgrades and removals
Configure Intune or SIEM to alert on unauthorized/unsupported installs
Document approved software list and review schedule

jordan.title: CIS 3.4 - Enforce Data Retention
jordan.notes: Define and enforce data retention policies - what data is kept, for how long, and where. Ensure automated disposal of data past retention period.

subtasks.title: Identify data types and storage locations (SharePoint, file servers, email, backups)
Define retention periods per data classification
Draft data retention policy document
Get policy approval from management
Configure retention policies in M365 / SharePoint
Configure backup retention to align with policy
Set up periodic review to verify compliance

jordan.title: CIS 4.2 - Enforce Unique Credentials
jordan.notes: Ensure every user and service account has unique credentials. Eliminate shared accounts and default passwords.

subtasks.title: Audit AD for shared or generic accounts
Audit service accounts for shared credentials
Assign unique credentials to all shared accounts or decommission them
Enforce password policy in AD (complexity, rotation, no reuse)
Document exceptions and justifications for any remaining shared accounts

jordan.title: CIS 4.6 - Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
jordan.notes: Ensure all externally-accessible applications and services require multi-factor authentication.

subtasks.title: Inventory all externally-exposed applications and services
Identify which already have MFA enforced
Enable MFA for M365 / Azure AD external access (Conditional Access)
Enable MFA for VPN access (ties into Duo/Meraki project)
Enable MFA for any remaining external-facing services
Verify MFA enforcement with test logins from external networks

jordan.title: CIS 4.7 - Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets
jordan.notes: Disable, rename, or change passwords on all default/built-in accounts across enterprise assets and software.

subtasks.title: Inventory default accounts on network devices (switches, firewalls, APs)
Inventory default accounts on servers and endpoints (local admin, guest)
Inventory default accounts in applications and services
Disable or rename default accounts where possible
Change default passwords on accounts that cannot be disabled
Configure Intune/GPO to enforce local admin account management (LAPS)
Document all changes and create recurring audit schedule
